session.c(3) Library Functions Manual session.c(3)

NAME

session.c

SYNOPSIS

Functions


discovery_session_t * discovery_session_create (const discovery_config_t *config)
Create a new discovery session. void discovery_session_destroy (discovery_session_t *session)
Destroy discovery session and free resources. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start (discovery_session_t *session)
Start the discovery session. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_process (discovery_session_t *session, int64_t timeout_ns)
Process session events (call in main loop). void discovery_session_stop (discovery_session_t *session)
Stop the discovery session. discovery_state_t discovery_session_get_state (const discovery_session_t *session)
Get current session state. bool discovery_session_is_active (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if session is active (call in progress). const char * discovery_session_get_string (const discovery_session_t *session)
Get session string. bool discovery_session_is_host (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if we are the host. session_host_t * discovery_session_get_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Get host context (if we are host). session_participant_t * discovery_session_get_participant (discovery_session_t *session)
Get participant context (if we are participant). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_init_ring (discovery_session_t *session)
Initialize ring consensus state. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start_ring_round (discovery_session_t *session)
Start a new ring consensus round (every 5 minutes or on new joiner). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_check_host_alive (discovery_session_t *session)
Detect host disconnect (check connection status). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_handle_host_disconnect (discovery_session_t *session, uint32_t disconnect_reason)
Handle host disconnect with automatic failover to future host. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_become_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Become the host (called when elected as future host). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_connect_to_future_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Connect to pre-elected future host (called when NOT future host). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_get_future_host (const discovery_session_t *session, uint8_t out_id[16], char out_address[64], uint16_t *out_port, uint8_t *out_connection_type)
Get future host information. bool discovery_session_is_future_host (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if we are the future host.

Function Documentation

Become the host (called when elected as future host).

Parameters

Session Session context

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success

2194   if (!session) {
2195     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2196     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2197   }
2198 
2199   log_info("Starting as new host after migration (participant ID: %02x%02x...)", session->participant_id[0],
2200            session->participant_id[1]);
2201 
2202   // Get configured port (or use default)
2203   int host_port = GET_OPTION(port);
2204 
2205   // Mark ourselves as host
2206   session->is_host = true;
2207 
2208   // Create host context if needed with configured port
2209   if (!session->host_ctx) {
2210     session_host_config_t hconfig = {
2211         .port = host_port,
2212         .ipv4_address = "0.0.0.0",
2213         .max_clients = 32,
2214         .encryption_enabled = true,
2215     };
2216 
2217     session->host_ctx = session_host_create(&hconfig);
2218     if (!session->host_ctx) {
2219       log_error("Failed to create host context for migration");
2220       return ERROR_MEMORY;
2221     }
2222 
2223     // Start listening for new participant connections
2224     asciichat_error_t hstart = session_host_start(session->host_ctx);
2225     if (hstart != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2226       log_error("Failed to start host after migration: %d", hstart);
2227       return hstart;
2228     }
2229 
2230     log_info("Host restarted after migration, listening on port %d", host_port);
2231   }
2232 
2233   // Send HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS so other participants can reconnect
2234   acip_host_announcement_t announcement = {0};
2235   memcpy(announcement.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
2236   memcpy(announcement.host_id, session->participant_id, 16);
2237   SAFE_STRNCPY(announcement.host_address, "127.0.0.1", sizeof(announcement.host_address));
2238   // TODO: Use actual determined host address instead of 127.0.0.1
2239   announcement.host_port = host_port;
2240   announcement.connection_type = ACIP_CONNECTION_TYPE_DIRECT_PUBLIC;
2241   // TODO: Use actual connection type based on NAT quality
2242 
2243   if (session->acds_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
2244     packet_send(session->acds_socket, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT, &announcement, sizeof(announcement));
2245     log_info("Sent HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS for new host %02x%02x... at %s:%u", announcement.host_id[0],
2246              announcement.host_id[1], announcement.host_address, announcement.host_port);
2247   }
2248 
2249   // Mark migration complete
2250   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2251 
2252   // Transition to ACTIVE state (host is ready)
2253   set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2254 
2255   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2256 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_check_host_alive
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Detect host disconnect (check connection status).


Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK if host alive, error if disconnected


Definition at line 2070 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2070                                                                                    {
2071   if (!session || session->is_host) {
2072     // We are the host, so "host" is always alive
2073     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2074   }
2075 
2076   // For WebRTC sessions, check the WebRTC transport instead of participant_ctx
2077   if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC) {
2078     // If we have a WebRTC transport and it's marked as ready, host is alive
2079     if (session->webrtc_transport_ready) {
2080       return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2081     }
2082     // WebRTC transport not ready yet - might still be connecting
2083     return ERROR_NETWORK;
2084   }
2085 
2086   // For TCP sessions, check participant context
2087   // If we don't have a participant context yet, we're not connected
2088   if (!session->participant_ctx) {
2089     return ERROR_NETWORK;
2090   }
2091 
2092   // Check migration state - if we're already migrating, host is definitely not alive
2093   if (session->migration.state != MIGRATION_STATE_NONE) {
2094     return ERROR_NETWORK; // Already detected disconnect
2095   }
2096 
2097   uint64_t now_ms = session_get_current_time_ms();
2098 
2099   // Check if we have a ping in flight that timed out
2100   if (session->liveness.ping_in_flight) {
2101     uint64_t ping_age_ms = now_ms - session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms;
2102     if (ping_age_ms > session->liveness.timeout_ms) {
2103       // Ping timed out
2104       session->liveness.consecutive_failures++;
2105       session->liveness.ping_in_flight = false;
2106       log_warn("Host ping timeout (attempt %u/%u, age=%llu ms)", session->liveness.consecutive_failures,
2107                session->liveness.max_failures, (unsigned long long)ping_age_ms);
2108 
2109       // Check if we've exceeded failure threshold
2110       if (session->liveness.consecutive_failures >= session->liveness.max_failures) {
2111         log_error("Host declared dead after %u consecutive ping failures", session->liveness.consecutive_failures);
2112         return ERROR_NETWORK;
2113       }
2114     }
2115   }
2116 
2117   // Check if it's time to send a new ping
2118   uint64_t time_since_last_ping = now_ms - session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms;
2119   if (!session->liveness.ping_in_flight && time_since_last_ping >= session->liveness.ping_interval_ms) {
2120     // Send ping to host via participant connection
2121     socket_t host_socket = session_participant_get_socket(session->participant_ctx);
2122     if (host_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
2123       asciichat_error_t result = packet_send(host_socket, PACKET_TYPE_PING, NULL, 0);
2124       if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2125         session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms = now_ms;
2126         session->liveness.ping_in_flight = true;
2127         log_debug("Sent ping to host (attempt %u/%u)", session->liveness.consecutive_failures + 1,
2128                   session->liveness.max_failures);
2129       } else {
2130         log_warn("Failed to send ping to host: %d", result);
2131         session->liveness.consecutive_failures++;
2132         if (session->liveness.consecutive_failures >= session->liveness.max_failures) {
2133           log_error("Host declared dead after %u consecutive send failures", session->liveness.consecutive_failures);
2134           return ERROR_NETWORK;
2135         }
2136       }
2137     }
2138   }
2139 
2140   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2141 }

2258                                                                                          {
2259   if (!session) {
2260     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2261     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2262   }
2263 
2264   log_info("Reconnecting to future host: %s:%u (connection type: %u)", session->ring.future_host_address,
2265            session->ring.future_host_port, session->ring.future_host_connection_type);
2266 
2267   // Destroy old participant context if present
2268   if (session->participant_ctx) {
2269     session_participant_destroy(session->participant_ctx);
2270     session->participant_ctx = NULL;
2271   }
2272 
2273   // Create new participant context for the future host
2274   session_participant_config_t pconfig = {
2275       .address = session->ring.future_host_address,
2276       .port = session->ring.future_host_port,
2277       .enable_audio = true,
2278       .enable_video = true,
2279       .encryption_enabled = true,
2280   };
2281 
2282   session->participant_ctx = session_participant_create(&pconfig);
2283   if (!session->participant_ctx) {
2284     log_error("Failed to create participant context for future host");
2285     return ERROR_MEMORY;
2286   }
2287 
2288   // Attempt connection to future host
2289   asciichat_error_t pconn = session_participant_connect(session->participant_ctx);
2290   if (pconn != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2291     log_error("Failed to connect to future host: %d", pconn);
2292     return pconn;
2293   }
2294 
2295   log_info("Connected to future host after migration");
2296 
2297   // Update our host info with the new future host details
2298   memcpy(session->host_id, session->ring.future_host_id, 16);
2299   SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->ring.future_host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
2300   session->host_port = session->ring.future_host_port;
2301 
2302   log_info("Updated host info to: %s:%u (id: %02x%02x...)", session->host_address, session->host_port,
2303            session->host_id[0], session->host_id[1]);
2304 
2305   // Mark migration complete (in real implementation, this would be done after successful connection)
2306   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2307 
2308   // Transition to ACTIVE state (participant is ready)
2309   set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2310 
2311   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2312 }

Create a new discovery session.


Parameters


Config Module Session
  configuration


Returns


New session or NULL on error


Definition at line 50 of file

50                                                                                 {
51   if (!config) {
52     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "config is NULL");
53     return NULL;
54   }
55 
56   discovery_session_t *session = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(discovery_session_t), discovery_session_t *);
57   if (!session) {
58     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate discovery session");
59     return NULL;
60   }
61 
62   session->state = DISCOVERY_STATE_INIT;
63   session->acds_socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
64   session->peer_manager = NULL;
65   session->webrtc_transport_ready = false;
66   session->webrtc_connection_initiated = false;
67   session->webrtc_retry_attempt = 0;
68   session->webrtc_last_attempt_time_ms = 0;
69   session->stun_servers = NULL;
70   session->stun_count = 0;
71   session->turn_servers = NULL;
72   session->turn_count = 0;
73 
74   // Initialize host liveness detection
75   session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms = 0;
76   session->liveness.last_pong_received_ms = 0;
77   session->liveness.consecutive_failures = 0;
78   session->liveness.max_failures = 3;
79   session->liveness.ping_interval_ms = 3 * MS_PER_SEC_INT; // Ping every 3 seconds
80   session->liveness.timeout_ms = 10 * MS_PER_SEC_INT;      // 10 second timeout
81   session->liveness.ping_in_flight = false;
82 
83   log_info("discovery_session_create: After CALLOC - participant_ctx=%p, host_ctx=%p", session->participant_ctx,
84            session->host_ctx);
85 
86   // Load or generate identity key
87   char identity_path[256];
88   asciichat_error_t id_result = acds_identity_default_path(identity_path, sizeof(identity_path));
89   if (id_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
90     id_result = acds_identity_load(identity_path, session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
91     if (id_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
92       // File doesn't exist or is corrupted - generate new key
93       log_info("Identity key not found, generating new key");
94       id_result = acds_identity_generate(session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
95       if (id_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
96         // Save for future use
97         acds_identity_save(identity_path, session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
98       }
99     }
100   }
101 
102   if (id_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
103     log_warn("Failed to load/generate identity key, using zero key");
104     memset(session->identity_pubkey, 0, sizeof(session->identity_pubkey));
105     memset(session->identity_seckey, 0, sizeof(session->identity_seckey));
106   } else {
107     char fingerprint[65];
108     acds_identity_fingerprint(session->identity_pubkey, fingerprint);
109     log_info("Using identity key with fingerprint: %.16s...", fingerprint);
110   }
111 
112   // Load multiple identity keys from options (multi-key protocol support)
113   const options_t *opts = options_get();
114   if (opts && opts->num_identity_keys > 0) {
115     // Load all identity keys from options
116     for (size_t i = 0; i < opts->num_identity_keys && i < MAX_IDENTITY_KEYS; i++) {
117       uint8_t pubkey[32] = {0};
118       uint8_t seckey[64] = {0};
119       asciichat_error_t key_result = acds_identity_load(opts->identity_keys[i], pubkey, seckey);
120       if (key_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
121         memcpy(session->identity_pubkeys[i], pubkey, 32);
122         char fingerprint[65];
123         acds_identity_fingerprint(pubkey, fingerprint);
124         log_info("Loaded identity key #%zu with fingerprint: %.16s...", i + 1, fingerprint);
125       } else {
126         log_warn("Failed to load identity key #%zu from %s", i + 1, opts->identity_keys[i]);
127       }
128     }
129     session->num_identity_keys = opts->num_identity_keys;
130     log_info("Loaded %zu identity key(s) for multi-key protocol", session->num_identity_keys);
131   } else {
132     // Single-key mode: use only the primary identity key
133     memcpy(session->identity_pubkeys[0], session->identity_pubkey, 32);
134     session->num_identity_keys = 1;
135   }
136 
137   // ACDS connection info
138   if (config->acds_address && config->acds_address[0]) {
139     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_address, config->acds_address, sizeof(session->acds_address));
140   } else {
141     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_address, "127.0.0.1", sizeof(session->acds_address));
142   }
143   session->acds_port = config->acds_port > 0 ? config->acds_port : ACIP_DISCOVERY_DEFAULT_PORT;
144 
145   // Session string (if joining)
146   if (config->session_string && config->session_string[0]) {
147     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->session_string, config->session_string, sizeof(session->session_string));
148     session->is_initiator = false;
149   } else {
150     session->is_initiator = true;
151   }
152 
153   // Callbacks
154   session->on_state_change = config->on_state_change;
155   session->on_session_ready = config->on_session_ready;
156   session->on_error = config->on_error;
157   session->callback_user_data = config->callback_user_data;
158   session->should_exit_callback = config->should_exit_callback;
159   session->exit_callback_data = config->exit_callback_data;
160 
161   log_debug("Discovery session created (initiator=%d, acds=%s:%u)", session->is_initiator, session->acds_address,
162             session->acds_port);
163 
164   return session;
165 }

187     session_participant_destroy(session->participant_ctx);

196

198   if (session->stun_servers) {
199     SAFE_FREE(session->stun_servers);

203 
204   if (session->turn_servers) {
205     SAFE_FREE(session->turn_servers);

207     session->turn_count = 0;

209

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_get_future_host (const
  discovery_session_t * session, uint8_t out_id[16], char
  out_address[64], uint16_t * out_port, uint8_t *
  out_connection_type)


Get future host information.


Parameters

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_handle_host_disconnect
  (discovery_session_t * session, uint32_t disconnect_reason)


Handle host disconnect with automatic failover to future host.


Parameters


Session Session context


disconnect_reason Reason code for disconnect


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on successful failover


Definition at line 2143 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2143                                                                                                                      {
2144   if (!session) {
2145     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2146     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2147   }
2148 
2149   log_warn("Host disconnect detected: reason=%u", disconnect_reason);
2150 
2151   // Initialize migration context
2152   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED;
2153   session->migration.detection_time_ms = session_get_current_time_ms();
2154   memcpy(session->migration.last_host_id, session->host_id, 16);
2155   session->migration.disconnect_reason = disconnect_reason;
2156 
2157   // Notify ACDS of host loss (lightweight notification, no NAT data exchanged!)
2158   acip_host_lost_t host_lost = {0};
2159   memcpy(host_lost.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
2160   memcpy(host_lost.participant_id, session->participant_id, 16);
2161   memcpy(host_lost.last_host_id, session->host_id, 16);
2162   host_lost.disconnect_reason = disconnect_reason;
2163   host_lost.disconnect_time_ms = session->migration.detection_time_ms;
2164 
2165   packet_send(session->acds_socket, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_LOST, &host_lost, sizeof(host_lost));
2166 
2167   // Check if we have a pre-elected future host (from last 5-minute ring round)
2168   if (session->ring.future_host_id[0] == 0) {
2169     // No future host known! This shouldn't happen if we just received FUTURE_HOST_ELECTED
2170     // within the last 5 minutes. Fall back to treating this as fatal.
2171     log_error("No future host pre-elected! Session cannot recover from host disconnect.");
2172     session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2173     return ERROR_NETWORK; // Session should end
2174   }
2175 
2176   // **SIMPLIFIED**: No metrics exchange needed! Future host was already elected 5 minutes ago.
2177   // Just failover to the pre-elected host.
2178   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER;
2179 
2180   // Check if I am the future host
2181   if (session->ring.am_future_host) {
2182     // I become the new host immediately! (no election delay!)
2183     log_info("Becoming host (I'm the pre-elected future host)");
2184     return discovery_session_become_host(session);
2185   } else {
2186     // Connect to pre-elected future host (address already stored!)
2187     log_info("Connecting to pre-elected future host: %s:%u", session->ring.future_host_address,
2188              session->ring.future_host_port);
2189     return discovery_session_connect_to_future_host(session);
2190   }
2191 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_process
  (discovery_session_t * session, int64_t timeout_ns)


Process session events (call in main loop). Handles incoming ACDS
    messages, negotiation, and state transitions.




Parameters


Session Session context


timeout_ns Max time to wait for events in nanoseconds (0 =
  non-blocking)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error on failure


Definition at line 1369 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


1369                                                                                               {
1370   if (!session) {
1371     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
1372   }
1373 
1374   // Handle state-specific processing
1375   switch (session->state) {
1376   case DISCOVERY_STATE_WAITING_PEER: {
1377     // Wait for PARTICIPANT_JOINED notification from ACDS
1378     if (session->acds_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE && timeout_ns > 0) {
1379       // Set socket timeout
1380       if (socket_set_timeout(session->acds_socket, timeout_ns) == 0) {
1381         packet_type_t type;
1382         void *data = NULL;
1383         size_t len = 0;
1384 
1385         // Try to receive a packet (non-blocking with timeout)
1386         asciichat_error_t recv_result = packet_receive(session->acds_socket, &type, &data, &len);
1387         if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1388           if (type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_PARTICIPANT_JOINED) {
1389             log_info("Peer joined session, transitioning to negotiation");
1390             set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING);
1391             POOL_FREE(data, len);
1392           } else {
1393             // Got some other packet, handle it or ignore
1394             log_debug("Received packet type %d while waiting for peer", type);
1395             POOL_FREE(data, len);
1396           }
1397         } else if (recv_result == ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT) {
1398           // Timeout is normal, just return
1399         } else {
1400           log_debug("Packet receive error while waiting for peer: %d", recv_result);
1401         }
1402       }
1403     } else {
1404       // Fallback to sleep if no timeout or invalid socket
1405       platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1406     }
1407     break;
1408   }
1409 
1410   case DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING: {
1411     // Handle NAT negotiation
1412     if (session->negotiate.state == NEGOTIATE_STATE_INIT) {
1413       // Initialize negotiation
1414       negotiate_init(&session->negotiate, session->session_id, session->participant_id, session->is_initiator);
1415       log_info("Starting NAT negotiation (initiator=%d)", session->is_initiator);
1416 
1417       // Send our NETWORK_QUALITY to ACDS immediately (let peer receive it)
1418       asciichat_error_t send_result = send_network_quality_to_acds(session);
1419       if (send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1420         log_warn("Failed to send initial NETWORK_QUALITY: %d", send_result);
1421         // Don't fail - we'll try again next iteration
1422       }
1423     }
1424 
1425     // Try to receive peer's NETWORK_QUALITY from ACDS (non-blocking with short timeout)
1426     if (!session->negotiate.peer_quality_received) {
1427       asciichat_error_t recv_result = receive_network_quality_from_acds(session);
1428       if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1429         // Got peer quality - proceed to election
1430         log_info("Received peer NETWORK_QUALITY, proceeding to host election");
1431       } else if (recv_result != ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT) {
1432         // Real error (not just timeout)
1433         log_debug("Error receiving NETWORK_QUALITY: %d (this might be normal if peer not ready)", recv_result);
1434       }
1435     }
1436 
1437     // Check if we have both qualities and can determine host
1438     if (session->negotiate.peer_quality_received && session->negotiate.state != NEGOTIATE_STATE_COMPARING) {
1439       // Both sides have exchanged NAT quality - determine the winner
1440       log_info("Both NAT qualities received, determining host...");
1441       asciichat_error_t result = negotiate_determine_result(&session->negotiate);
1442       if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1443         // Store result
1444         session->is_host = session->negotiate.we_are_host;
1445 
1446         if (session->is_host) {
1447           // We won the negotiation - become the host
1448           log_info("🏠 NAT negotiation complete: WE ARE HOST (tier:%d vs tier:%d)",
1449                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.our_quality),
1450                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.peer_quality));
1451           memcpy(session->host_id, session->participant_id, 16);
1452           SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->negotiate.host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
1453           session->host_port = session->negotiate.host_port;
1454           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST);
1455         } else {
1456           // They won - we'll be a participant
1457           log_info("👤 NAT negotiation complete: THEY ARE HOST at %s:%u (tier:%d vs tier:%d)",
1458                    session->negotiate.host_address, session->negotiate.host_port,
1459                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.our_quality),
1460                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.peer_quality));
1461           SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->negotiate.host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
1462           session->host_port = session->negotiate.host_port;
1463           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST);
1464         }
1465       } else {
1466         log_error("Failed to determine negotiation result: %d", result);
1467         set_error(session, result, "Host election failed");
1468       }
1469     } else {
1470       // Still waiting for peer quality - sleep briefly to avoid busy loop
1471       platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1472     }
1473     break;
1474   }
1475 
1476   case DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST: {
1477     // Start hosting
1478     if (!session->host_ctx) {
1479       // Create host context with configured port
1480       int host_port = GET_OPTION(port);
1481 
1482       session_host_config_t hconfig = {
1483           .port = host_port,
1484           .ipv4_address = "0.0.0.0",
1485           .max_clients = 32,
1486           .encryption_enabled = true,
1487       };
1488 
1489       session->host_ctx = session_host_create(&hconfig);
1490       if (!session->host_ctx) {
1491         log_error("Failed to create host context");
1492         set_error(session, ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create host context");
1493         break;
1494       }
1495 
1496       // Start listening
1497       asciichat_error_t hstart = session_host_start(session->host_ctx);
1498       if (hstart != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1499         log_error("Failed to start host: %d", hstart);
1500         set_error(session, hstart, "Failed to start host");
1501         break;
1502       }
1503 
1504       log_info("Host started, listening for connections");
1505     }
1506 
1507     // Notify listeners that we're ready
1508     if (session->on_session_ready) {
1509       session->on_session_ready(session->session_string, session->callback_user_data);
1510     }
1511 
1512     set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1513     break;
1514   }
1515 
1516   case DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST: {
1517     // Connect to host as participant
1518 
1519     // For WebRTC sessions, wait for DataChannel to open
1520     if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC) {
1521       // Check if transport is ready (DataChannel opened)
1522       if (session->webrtc_transport_ready) {
1523         log_info("WebRTC DataChannel established, transitioning to ACTIVE state");
1524         set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1525         break;
1526       }
1527 
1528       // If not ready and we haven't initiated connection yet, initiate it
1529       if (session->peer_manager && !session->webrtc_connection_initiated) {
1530         log_info("Initiating WebRTC connection to host (attempt %d/%d)...", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1531                  GET_OPTION(webrtc_reconnect_attempts) + 1);
1532 
1533         // Initiate connection (generates offer, triggers SDP exchange)
1534         asciichat_error_t conn_result =
1535             webrtc_peer_manager_connect(session->peer_manager, session->session_id, session->host_id);
1536 
1537         if (conn_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1538           log_error("Failed to initiate WebRTC connection: %d", conn_result);
1539           set_error(session, conn_result, "Failed to initiate WebRTC connection");
1540           break;
1541         }
1542 
1543         log_info("WebRTC connection initiated, waiting for DataChannel...");
1544         session->webrtc_connection_initiated = true;
1545         session->webrtc_last_attempt_time_ms = platform_get_monotonic_time_us() / 1000;
1546       }
1547 
1548       // Check for ICE gathering timeout on all peers
1549       if (session->webrtc_connection_initiated && session->peer_manager) {
1550         int timeout_ms = GET_OPTION(webrtc_ice_timeout_ms);
1551         int timed_out_count = webrtc_peer_manager_check_gathering_timeouts(session->peer_manager, timeout_ms);
1552 
1553         if (timed_out_count > 0) {
1554           log_error("ICE gathering timeout: %d peer(s) failed to gather candidates within %dms", timed_out_count,
1555                     timeout_ms);
1556 
1557           // Check if we have retries remaining
1558           int max_attempts = GET_OPTION(webrtc_reconnect_attempts);
1559           if (session->webrtc_retry_attempt < max_attempts) {
1560             // Calculate exponential backoff delay
1561             uint32_t backoff_ms = calculate_backoff_delay_ms(session->webrtc_retry_attempt);
1562 
1563             log_warn("WebRTC connection attempt %d/%d failed, retrying in %ums...", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1564                      max_attempts + 1, backoff_ms);
1565 
1566             // Wait for backoff period
1567             platform_sleep_ms(backoff_ms);
1568 
1569             // Destroy old peer manager
1570             webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(session->peer_manager);
1571             session->peer_manager = NULL;
1572 
1573             // Re-initialize peer manager for retry
1574             asciichat_error_t reinit_result = initialize_webrtc_peer_manager(session);
1575             if (reinit_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1576               log_error("Failed to re-initialize WebRTC for retry: %d", reinit_result);
1577               set_error(session, reinit_result, "Failed to re-initialize WebRTC for retry");
1578 
1579               // If --prefer-webrtc is set, this is a fatal error
1580               if (GET_OPTION(prefer_webrtc)) {
1581                 log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1582                 set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1583                 return ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT;
1584               }
1585 
1586               break; // Exit this case to allow fallback to TCP
1587             }
1588 
1589             // Reset connection state for retry
1590             session->webrtc_connection_initiated = false;
1591             session->webrtc_retry_attempt++;
1592 
1593             log_info("WebRTC peer manager re-initialized for attempt %d/%d", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1594                      max_attempts + 1);
1595           } else {
1596             // No more retries - connection failed
1597             log_error("WebRTC connection failed after %d attempts (timeout: %dms)", max_attempts + 1, timeout_ms);
1598 
1599             // Set error state - WebRTC connection failed
1600             char error_msg[256];
1601             snprintf(error_msg, sizeof(error_msg), "WebRTC connection failed after %d attempts (%dms timeout)",
1602                      max_attempts + 1, timeout_ms);
1603             set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT, error_msg);
1604 
1605             // If --prefer-webrtc is set, this is a fatal error (no TCP fallback)
1606             if (GET_OPTION(prefer_webrtc)) {
1607               log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1608               set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1609               return ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT;
1610             }
1611 
1612             break; // Exit this case to allow fallback to TCP
1613           }
1614         }
1615       }
1616 
1617       // Poll ACDS for WebRTC signaling messages (SDP answer, ICE candidates)
1618       // while waiting for DataChannel to open
1619       if (session->acds_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1620         // Check if data available (non-blocking with timeout_ns)
1621         struct timeval tv_timeout;
1622         tv_timeout.tv_sec = timeout_ns / NS_PER_SEC_INT;
1623         tv_timeout.tv_usec = (timeout_ns % NS_PER_SEC_INT) / 1000;
1624 
1625         fd_set readfds;
1626         FD_ZERO(&readfds);
1627         FD_SET(session->acds_socket, &readfds);
1628 
1629         int select_result = select(session->acds_socket + 1, &readfds, NULL, NULL, &tv_timeout);
1630         if (select_result > 0 && FD_ISSET(session->acds_socket, &readfds)) {
1631           // Data available to read
1632           packet_type_t type;
1633           void *data = NULL;
1634           size_t len = 0;
1635 
1636           asciichat_error_t recv_result = packet_receive(session->acds_socket, &type, &data, &len);
1637 
1638           if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1639             // Dispatch to appropriate handler
1640             handle_acds_webrtc_packet(session, type, data, len);
1641             POOL_FREE(data, len);
1642           } else {
1643             log_debug("Failed to receive ACDS packet: %d", recv_result);
1644           }
1645         }
1646       }
1647 
1648       // Stay in CONNECTING_HOST until DataChannel opens (webrtc_transport_ready becomes true)
1649       // The discovery_on_transport_ready callback will set webrtc_transport_ready=true
1650       break;
1651     }
1652 
1653     if (!session->participant_ctx) {
1654       // Create participant context for this session
1655       session_participant_config_t pconfig = {
1656           .address = session->host_address,
1657           .port = session->host_port,
1658           .enable_audio = true,
1659           .enable_video = true,
1660           .encryption_enabled = true,
1661       };
1662 
1663       session->participant_ctx = session_participant_create(&pconfig);
1664       if (!session->participant_ctx) {
1665         log_error("Failed to create participant context");
1666         set_error(session, ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create participant context");
1667         break;
1668       }
1669 
1670       // Attempt connection
1671       asciichat_error_t pconn = session_participant_connect(session->participant_ctx);
1672       if (pconn != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1673         log_error("Failed to connect as participant: %d", pconn);
1674         // Stay in this state, will retry on next process() call
1675         break;
1676       }
1677 
1678       log_info("Connected to host, performing crypto handshake...");
1679 
1680       // Perform crypto handshake with server
1681       // Get the socket from participant context
1682       socket_t participant_socket = session_participant_get_socket(session->participant_ctx);
1683       if (participant_socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1684         log_error("Failed to get socket from participant context");
1685         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to get participant socket");
1686         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1687         break;
1688       }
1689 
1690       // Step 1: Send client protocol version
1691       log_debug("Sending protocol version to server...");
1692       protocol_version_packet_t client_version = {0};
1693       client_version.protocol_version = HOST_TO_NET_U16(1);  // Protocol version 1
1694       client_version.protocol_revision = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0); // Revision 0
1695       client_version.supports_encryption = 1;                // We support encryption
1696       client_version.compression_algorithms = 0;
1697       client_version.compression_threshold = 0;
1698       client_version.feature_flags = 0;
1699 
1700       int result = send_protocol_version_packet(participant_socket, &client_version);
1701       if (result != 0) {
1702         log_error("Failed to send protocol version to server");
1703         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send protocol version");
1704         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1705         break;
1706       }
1707 
1708       // Step 2: Receive server protocol version
1709       log_debug("Receiving server protocol version...");
1710       packet_type_t packet_type;
1711       void *payload = NULL;
1712       size_t payload_len = 0;
1713 
1714       int recv_result = receive_packet(participant_socket, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_len);
1715       if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK || packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION) {
1716         log_error("Failed to receive server protocol version (got type 0x%x)", packet_type);
1717         if (payload) {
1718           buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1719         }
1720         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to receive protocol version from server");
1721         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1722         break;
1723       }
1724 
1725       if (payload_len != sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t)) {
1726         log_error("Invalid protocol version packet size: %zu", payload_len);
1727         buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1728         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Invalid protocol version packet");
1729         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1730         break;
1731       }
1732 
1733       protocol_version_packet_t server_version;
1734       memcpy(&server_version, payload, sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t));
1735       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1736 
1737       if (!server_version.supports_encryption) {
1738         log_error("Server does not support encryption");
1739         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Server does not support encryption");
1740         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1741         break;
1742       }
1743 
1744       log_info("Server protocol version: %u.%u (encryption: yes)", NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_version),
1745                NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_revision));
1746 
1747       // Step 3: Send crypto capabilities
1748       log_debug("Sending crypto capabilities...");
1749       crypto_capabilities_packet_t client_caps = {0};
1750       client_caps.supported_kex_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0001);    // KEX_ALGO_X25519
1751       client_caps.supported_auth_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0003);   // AUTH_ALGO_ED25519 | AUTH_ALGO_NONE
1752       client_caps.supported_cipher_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0001); // CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305
1753       client_caps.requires_verification = 0;
1754       client_caps.preferred_kex = 0x0001;    // KEX_ALGO_X25519
1755       client_caps.preferred_auth = 0x0001;   // AUTH_ALGO_ED25519
1756       client_caps.preferred_cipher = 0x0001; // CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305
1757 
1758       result = send_crypto_capabilities_packet(participant_socket, &client_caps);
1759       if (result != 0) {
1760         log_error("Failed to send crypto capabilities");
1761         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send crypto capabilities");
1762         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1763         break;
1764       }
1765 
1766       log_info("Crypto handshake initiated successfully");
1767       log_warn("*** Connected to host as participant - transitioning to ACTIVE ***");
1768     }
1769 
1770     // Check if connection is established
1771     bool ctx_valid = session->participant_ctx != NULL;
1772     bool is_connected = ctx_valid ? session_participant_is_connected(session->participant_ctx) : false;
1773     log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - participant_ctx=%p, ctx_valid=%d, is_connected=%d",
1774               session->participant_ctx, ctx_valid, is_connected);
1775 
1776     if (ctx_valid && !is_connected) {
1777       // Log more details about why it's not connected
1778       log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - context exists but not connected yet");
1779     }
1780 
1781     if (is_connected) {
1782       log_info("Participant connection confirmed, transitioning to ACTIVE state");
1783       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1784     } else {
1785       log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - awaiting connection establishment (ctx_valid=%d, "
1786                 "is_connected=%d)",
1787                 ctx_valid, is_connected);
1788     }
1789     break;
1790   }
1791 
1792   case DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE:
1793     // Receive and handle ACDS packets for WebRTC signaling (NEW)
1794     if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC && session->acds_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1795       // Use non-blocking polling to check for incoming ACDS packets
1796       struct pollfd pfd = {.fd = session->acds_socket, .events = POLLIN, .revents = 0};
1797 
1798       int poll_result = socket_poll(&pfd, 1, 0); // Non-blocking poll (timeout=0)
1799 
1800       if (poll_result > 0 && (pfd.revents & POLLIN)) {
1801         // Data available to read
1802         packet_type_t type;
1803         void *data = NULL;
1804         size_t len = 0;
1805 
1806         asciichat_error_t recv_result = packet_receive(session->acds_socket, &type, &data, &len);
1807 
1808         if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1809           // Dispatch to appropriate handler
1810           handle_acds_webrtc_packet(session, type, data, len);
1811           POOL_FREE(data, len);
1812         }
1813       }
1814     }
1815 
1816     // Session is active - check for host disconnect
1817     if (!session->is_host) {
1818       // We are a participant - check if host is still alive
1819       asciichat_error_t host_check = discovery_session_check_host_alive(session);
1820       if (host_check != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1821         // Host is down! Trigger automatic failover to pre-elected future host
1822         log_warn("Host disconnect detected during ACTIVE state");
1823 
1824         // If --prefer-webrtc is set, WebRTC failure is fatal (no TCP fallback)
1825         const options_t *opts = options_get();
1826         if (opts && opts->prefer_webrtc) {
1827           log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1828           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1829           session->error = ERROR_NETWORK;
1830           return ERROR_NETWORK;
1831         }
1832 
1833         set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING);
1834         // Reason code: 1 = timeout/disconnect detected during ACTIVE session
1835         discovery_session_handle_host_disconnect(session, 1);
1836       }
1837     }
1838     platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1839     break;
1840 
1841   case DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING:
1842     // Host migration in progress
1843     // Check if migration has timed out (if it takes >30 seconds, give up)
1844     if (session->migration.state == MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE) {
1845       // Migration completed successfully
1846       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1847       log_info("Migration complete, session resumed");
1848     } else if (session_get_current_time_ms() - session->migration.detection_time_ms > 30 * MS_PER_SEC_INT) {
1849       // Migration timed out after 30 seconds
1850       log_error("Host migration timeout - session cannot recover");
1851       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1852       session->error = ERROR_NETWORK;
1853     }
1854     platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1855     break;
1856 
1857   default:
1858     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid session state");
1859     break;
1860   }
1861 
1862   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1863 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Start the discovery session. Connects to ACDS and either creates
    or joins a session.




Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success

1265                                                                         {
1266   log_info("discovery_session_start: ENTRY - session=%p", session);
1267 
1268   if (!session) {
1269     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
1270   }
1271 
1272   log_info("discovery_session_start: is_initiator=%d, session_string='%s'", session->is_initiator,
1273            session->session_string);
1274 
1275   // Check if we should exit before starting blocking operations
1276   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1277     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (before ACDS connect)");
1278     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1279   }
1280 
1281   log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling connect_to_acds...");
1282   // Connect to ACDS
1283   asciichat_error_t result = connect_to_acds(session);
1284   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1285     log_error("discovery_session_start: connect_to_acds FAILED - result=%d", result);
1286     return result;
1287   }
1288   log_info("discovery_session_start: connect_to_acds succeeded");
1289 
1290   // Check again after connection
1291   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1292     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (after ACDS connect)");
1293     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1294   }
1295 
1296   // Perform crypto handshake with ACDS (same as client-server handshake)
1297   log_info("discovery_session_start: Performing crypto handshake with ACDS...");
1298   crypto_handshake_context_t handshake_ctx;
1299   memset(&handshake_ctx, 0, sizeof(handshake_ctx));
1300 
1301   result = crypto_handshake_init("discovery_client", &handshake_ctx, false);
1302   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1303     log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake init failed - %s", asciichat_error_string(result));
1304     set_error(session, result, "Crypto handshake init failed");
1305     return result;
1306   }
1307 
1308   // Store identity key in handshake context
1309   handshake_ctx.client_public_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
1310   handshake_ctx.client_private_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
1311   memcpy(handshake_ctx.client_public_key.key, session->identity_pubkey, 32);
1312   memcpy(handshake_ctx.client_private_key.key.ed25519, session->identity_seckey, 64);
1313 
1314   // Step 1: Key exchange
1315   result = crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange_socket(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_socket);
1316   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1317     log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake key exchange failed");
1318     set_error(session, result, "Handshake key exchange failed");
1319     return result;
1320   }
1321 
1322   // Step 2: Auth response
1323   // Note: For ACDS servers without authentication, auth_response completes the handshake
1324   result = crypto_handshake_client_auth_response_socket(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_socket);
1325   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1326     log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake auth response failed");
1327     set_error(session, result, "Handshake auth response failed");
1328     return result;
1329   }
1330 
1331   // Step 3: Complete handshake
1332   // Note: Some servers (like ACDS without auth) complete the handshake in step 2,
1333   // so check if we're already done before trying to receive another packet
1334   if (handshake_ctx.state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY) {
1335     result = crypto_handshake_client_complete_socket(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_socket);
1336     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1337       log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake complete failed");
1338       set_error(session, result, "Handshake complete failed");
1339       return result;
1340     }
1341   } else {
1342     log_info("discovery_session_start: Handshake already complete from auth_response step");
1343   }
1344 
1345   log_info("discovery_session_start: Crypto handshake with ACDS completed");
1346 
1347   // Check again after handshake
1348   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1349     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (after handshake)");
1350     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1351   }
1352 
1353   // Create or join session
1354   if (session->is_initiator) {
1355     log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling create_session - participant_ctx before=%p", session->participant_ctx);
1356     result = create_session(session);
1357     log_info("discovery_session_start: create_session returned - participant_ctx after=%p, result=%d",
1358              session->participant_ctx, result);
1359     return result;
1360   } else {
1361     log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling join_session - participant_ctx before=%p", session->participant_ctx);
1362     asciichat_error_t result = join_session(session);
1363     log_info("discovery_session_start: join_session returned - participant_ctx after=%p, state=%d, result=%d",
1364              session->participant_ctx, session->state, result);
1365     return result;
1366   }
1367 }

    joiner).


Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success

2045   }
2046

2050     log_info("Starting 5-minute proactive election round (collecting NETWORK_QUALITY from participants)");
2051 
2052     // HOST: Run proactive host election
2053     // 1. Collect NETWORK_QUALITY from all connected participants (TODO: request fresh data)
2054     // 2. Measure the host's own NAT quality
2055     // 3. Run deterministic election to determine future host
2056     // 4. Broadcast FUTURE_HOST_ELECTED to all participants via ACDS

2058     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2059       log_error("Host election failed: %d", result);
2060       return result;
2061     }
2062 
2063     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2064   } else {

void
  discovery_session_stop (discovery_session_t * session)


Stop the discovery session.


Parameters


Session Session context


Definition at line 1865 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


1865                                                           {
1866   if (!session) {
1867     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
1868     return;
1869   }
1870 
1871   log_info("Stopping discovery session...");
1872 
1873   // Send SESSION_LEAVE if connected
1874   if (session->acds_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE && session->session_id[0] != 0) {
1875     acip_session_leave_t leave_msg;
1876     memset(&leave_msg, 0, sizeof(leave_msg));
1877     memcpy(leave_msg.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
1878     memcpy(leave_msg.participant_id, session->participant_id, 16);
1879 
1880     packet_send(session->acds_socket, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_SESSION_LEAVE, &leave_msg, sizeof(leave_msg));
1881   }
1882 
1883   set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ENDED);
1884 }
